summary: There is no demonstrable difference in deflation rates between prefilled and standard textured saline breast implants due to shared underlying mechanisms and absence of studies showing varied leakage patterns based on fill type. Both implant types exhibit similar defect profiles related to age, trauma, or other non-manufacturing variables, making any distinction unproven.
